description: SQL LSP / language server: positioned diagnostics, typed hover, go-to-definition, completion, signature help, semantic tokens. Databricks, T-SQL, Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres, DuckDB, Trino.
rules: The server requires an explicit transport flag on every launch: sqllens-language-server --stdio
Dialect is configured, never guessed: .sqllens.json at the workspace root maps file globs to dialects with a default; without it every file falls back to the databricks dialect

The schema catalog ({ "table": { "column": "type" } }, referenced from .sqllens.json) enables unknown-column diagnostics, typed hover and schema-aware completion; without it only the syntax tiers run — that silence is by design, not a bug
In VS Code install the sqllens-sql extension (server bundled); in Claude Code install the sqllens-lsp plugin AND npm install -g sqllens-language-server (the plugin does not bundle the server)
For programmatic SQL analysis use the sqllens library directly; this package is the LSP protocol adapter over it
